latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next

latest.title: Lions hunt better when the pride coordinates
latest.script_key: lions turn hunting into a team problem watch the spacing before the chase because lions can use different positions in a group hunt one animal pressures while another blocks the escape path the power is not just muscle which predator tactic should we break down next

latest.title: Colobus monkeys survive with acrobatic forest tricks
latest.script_key: check these acrobats colobus monkeys never touch the ground their legs stretch like springs to leap 30 feet between trees that s why they re safe from leopards watch how their tails steer mid air like a rudder could you handle a 20 foot gap

latest.title: Tawny frogmouths aren't owls but they hunt like them
latest.script_key: this bird isn t an owl that s why it perches like a statue watch that beak it points straight up not down like an owl s that s camouflage it blends into the bark then strikes did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Forests make cooler air under the canopy
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next

latest.title: Lions have dark ear marks cubs can follow
latest.script_key: lions have dark ear marks cubs can follow watch the backs of the ears because those dark marks can stand out when a lion moves through grass for cubs that contrast helps track the adult ahead the detail is small but the pride depends on staying together would you spot it

latest.title: Owls don't blink like you think
latest.script_key: this owl never blinks notice the third eyelid sliding sideways like a window owls have a nictitating membrane that cleans and protects the eye without blocking vision that s why they can stay locked on prey for minutes did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Ocean waves aren't just water sloshing
latest.script_key: you d think waves just crash and erode but look see how the water curls that s not random the crash compresses air so fast it blasts tiny holes in the stone that s why tide pools form next time you see a smooth rock that s not erosion it s millions of micro explosions
